title: coelachyrum indicum
intro: Coelachyrum indicum, also known as Indian buttonbush, is a flowering plant species native to the Indian subcontinent. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurveda, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, providing benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Coelachyrum indicum is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
